lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Why howdy there, sailor! Welcome to this here fine shipyard. Anything I can help ya with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about this place?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Why, this is the shipyard, of course! I'd say it's the finest one in all of Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How many others even are there in Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Ha! You're a funny one, sailor! Anyhoo, this here is the place where ya can modify your boat to your heart's content! Ya can do that in two ways.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Once you're on board, you'll find that you've got different spots on your boat for different facilities! If ya can't find what you're looking for, be sure to check the other spots as well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what if I don't have the right skillsets?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Well now, I reckon we might be able to help ya out there... for a price of course!

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Now, the other way ya can modify your boat is by upgrading the core components of it. If ya fancy doing that, you'll wanna use this here schematics board!

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Much like with building facilities, ya can use the board to pick the part of your boat ya wanna upgrade!

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Now, it's important ya keep in mind that these upgrades are the big ones! They're too much to handle solo, so we'll wrangle up some of the workers to lend ya a hand. They'll also supply the tools for ya!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's very kind of them. What's the catch?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Well, while they're happy to give ya a helping hand, they are going to expect ya to take the lead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And if I'm not skilled enough to take the lead?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Well, we might be able to help ya out there... for a price of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Anyhoo, I think that just about covers the shipyard. Anything else I can do for ya?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you have for sale?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: I've got a few basic shipbuilding supplies that ya might find handy. Take a look, sailor!

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Looks like ya boat's already all fine and dandy, sailor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you help me repair my boat?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Sure thing, sailor. That'll cost ya [..] coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Alrighty! Let's call some of the workers over...

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: That's all done for ya, sailor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Alrighty! Let's call some of the workers over...

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: That's all done for ya, sailor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'd rather not.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Fair enough, sailor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get materials for my boat?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Well, ain't that the question on everyone's mind, sailor? But to answer, it depends on what materials you'll be wanting.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Ya look like a smart enough sailor to know where to find logs and bars. Nails, ya make from taking a metal bar to an anvil.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: For planks, you'll need to take matching logs to a sawmill. I know there's a few around, but I like to get mine from the one north east of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: If ya need standard hull parts, you'll wanna take five planks to a workbench. We've got one right here, but any good shipwright's gonna have one.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: If it's standard keel parts ya need, you'll wanna take five metal bars to any old anvil.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: For the larger hull parts or keel parts, don't overthink it. Just grab five of the standard parts and put them together in the same way!

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Obviously, some raw materials are rarer than others, and some of them ya might need to go sail that great blue marble to get.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Finally, if it helps ya at all, I've got some basic supplies for sale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might need help building things on my boat.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Well, ya wouldn't be the first, sailor! I reckon ya probably won't be the the last either. Don't worry though, we can help ya out... for a price of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what sort of price should I expect?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Well, that there depends on how hard the job is. The more skills needed, the more money you're gonna need to part with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're telling me you won't be able to spare your best shipwrights?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Yeah I'd say that's a good way of putting it. In general, we'll only be able to help ya with things that use less fancy materials. Mithril and mahogany are gonna be the limit.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: We're also only gonna be able to help ya with the more standard parts and facilities, so hulls, sails, helms, keels, cannons, salvaging hooks and cargo holds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So once things start to get complicated, I'm going to have to do it myself?

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: Afraid so, sailor. Oh, and don't forget that even when we're helping ya out, we're still gonna need ya to supply the materials.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Shipwright Seb
lines.text: See ya later, sailor.
